sections.paragraph:
τρομέω
Etym. τρόμος

I. to tremble, quake, quiver, esp. from fear, Il.:—c. inf. to fear to do, Theocr.

II. c. acc. to tremble before or at a person, to stand in awe of, Hom.


Etym. In each sense Hom. uses both Act. and Mid., but only in pres. and imperf.; epic and ionic τρομεοίατο for τρομέοιντο, Il.; ionic part. τρομεύμενος Solon.
